description: Sio Sam Ong is a leading Chinese triad in Malaysia, based in Penang and Selangor, with strong presence in northern states like Kedah and Perlis. Established in the 1940s-1950s, it is considered one of the most powerful triads in the country, primarily composed of Hokkien ethnic group. The organization is involved in international drug trafficking, kidnapping, murder, extortion, racketeering, and loansharking, and has been linked to multiple murders in Penang in the late 1980s and early 1990s.
